claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n equals n.
predicate: (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all divisors d of n equals n, if n is prime.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all divisors d of n equals n, if n is a power of 2.
predicate: (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n) and (is_power_of_2(n))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all divisors d of n equals n, if n is a product of distinct primes.
predicate: (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n) and (not is_prime(n)) and (all(is_prime(p) and p != n for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all divisors d of n equals n, if n is a power of 2 or a prime.
predicate: (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n) and ((is_power_of_2(n)) or (is_prime(n)))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of the k-th powers of the divisors of n equals n^k * prod((p^i+1)/(p^(i+1)-1) for p in primes if n % p == 0) if k is even.
